action.reasoning: Observation: Waypoint controller yields safe_follow=0 in all test episodes. The robot never achieves the required angle-distance constraint simultaneously. The angle to human remains large (often >1 rad) even when distance is moderate. The pathfinder approach adds complexity but doesn't improve approach. Analysis: The fundamental issue is that the controller never rotates to face the human before moving forward, so safe_follow never accumulates. Decision: Implement a reactive controller that (1) turns in place when abs(angle) > 0.3, (2) approaches when facing and distance > target, (3) backs away if too close, (4) stays still within safe zone. Use pathfinder only for long-range guidance with in-place reorientation at waypoints. This should produce non-zero safe_follow. Update train.py (test 3 episodes) and predict.py (same logic), then submit eval.
